officialFindings.finding: The hyprctl keyword command sets a config keyword dynamically and returns ok on success or an error message on failure.
The hyprctl command list describes getoption as reading config option status and keyword as issuing a dynamic config keyword.

dispatchKeyword calls the legacy config manager parseKeyword and returns ok only when the returned parser string is empty; it also performs selected side effects for input, blur, decoration, workspace, rules, and source commands.
dispatchGetOption reads Config::mgr()->getConfigValue and reports typed values such as bool, int, float, str, custom type, and set status.
configerrors reads Config::mgr()->getErrors and formats the current config parsing errors.

level3Policy.strictPass: keyword succeeds, configerrors remains clean, getoption changes to the candidate value, and revert verification passes.
level3Policy.acceptedUnobservable: keyword succeeds, configerrors remains clean, revert verification passes, but getoption does not change to the candidate. This is not enough for automatic enablement.
level3Policy.rejected: keyword fails, keyword stderr is non-empty, or the command reports an error message.
level3Policy.configError: configerrors reports an error after apply.
level3Policy.requiresReload: official source or docs indicate the setting is not observable without reload. Reload is not run in this sprint.
level3Policy.notLiveChangeable: official source or docs indicate the setting is config-only or not dynamically applicable.
level3Policy.unproven: the evidence does not fit a stronger status, or revert verification is missing.

items.hyprctlKeywordStdout: keyword can't work with non-legacy parsers. Use eval.
items.reason: Keyword succeeded and configerrors stayed clean, but getoption did not change to the candidate. Strict live-observable proof is not met.
items.nextAction: keep-blocked; research config-persistence or per-setting observability before enabling
